result.summary: Databricks exposes workspace and account REST APIs, a Databricks CLI, and Databricks MCP servers; authentication is primarily OAuth (user or service-principal), with workspace PATs also supported for some surfaces.
result.description: Databricks provides a cloud data, analytics, and AI platform with workspace-level and account-level administration, data/ML services, and agent tooling. Its products include managed data/ML services, Unity Catalog governance, SQL, and AI agent integrations.

db_oauth_user.label: Databricks user OAuth app/client
db_oauth_user.setup: For user-authorized integrations, use Databricks OAuth as described in [Authorize user access to Databricks with OAuth](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/dev-tools/auth/oauth-u2m). For interactive tools, Databricks recommends unified authentication; for custom apps you can follow the manual OAuth flow on that page. For account-level operations set `DATABRICKS_HOST` to `https://accounts.cloud.databricks.com`; for workspace APIs set it to your workspace URL.

db_oauth_sp.label: Databricks service principal OAuth client credentials
db_oauth_sp.setup: Create a Databricks service principal and then create an OAuth secret as described in [Authorize service principal access to Databricks with OAuth](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/dev-tools/auth/oauth-m2m). Databricks recommends unified authentication for tools/SDKs; for custom integrations you can use the manual M2M token flow from that page. For account-level operations use `DATABRICKS_HOST` = `https://accounts.cloud.databricks.com`; for workspace APIs use your workspace URL.

db_pat.label: Databricks personal access token (workspace-level legacy token)
db_pat.setup: In your Databricks workspace, open **Settings** → **Developer** → **Access tokens** → **Manage** → **Generate new token** as documented in [Personal access tokens (legacy)](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/dev-tools/auth/pat). PATs are workspace-scoped and cannot be used for account-level APIs.

variables.name: workspace_host
variables.description: Workspace-level APIs are hosted at workspace URLs.

surfaces.name: Databricks MCP servers
surfaces.notes: Databricks documents MCP connectivity over Streamable HTTP and says clients authenticate with OAuth or a personal access token. The server URL is feature-specific (managed MCP server, MCP Service, or Databricks-hosted custom MCP) and must be obtained from the relevant product page; no single fixed connect endpoint was identified in the public docs.
